name: What to Do After a Machine Accident at Work: A Step-by-Step Guide for Utah Workers
description: What to Do After a Machine Accident at Work: A Step-by-Step Guide for Utah Workers - Utah County

step.name: Step 1
step.text: Getting injured by a heavy machine on a factory floor or construction site is a traumatic event that leaves you feeling vulnerable and uncertain about your future. In Utah and Idaho, the moments immediately following a machine accident are critical not only for your physical recovery but also for your legal rights. This guide outlines the essential steps you must take to protect your claim and ensure you receive the benefits you deserve.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Your health is the first priority. If you have suffered a crushing injury, amputation, or severe laceration, call 911 or head to the nearest emergency room in Salt Lake City, Ogden, or Boise. Do not wait until the end of your shift. A delay in seeking treatment gives the insurance company an excuse to claim the injury didn't happen at work. Tell the doctor exactly which machine caused the injury and how it happened.

step.name: Step 4
step.text: ### 2. Report the Accident to Your Supervisor

step.name: Step 5
step.text: In Utah, you must report a workplace injury within 30 days, while Idaho allows up to 60 days. However, you should report it immediately. Ensure the report is in writing—an email or a text message provides a timestamped record that your boss was notified. Be specific about the mechanical failure or lack of safety guards.

step.name: Step 6
step.text: ### 3. Document the Scene and the Machine

step.name: Step 7
step.text: If you are physically able, or if you have a trusted coworker, take photos of the machine that hurt you. Specifically, look for:

step.name: Step 9
step.text: - Warning labels that were obscured or removed.

step.name: Step 11
step.text: - Fluid leaks or broken parts on the floor.

step.name: Step 12
step.text: Industrial sites are cleaned and machines are repaired quickly after accidents. Taking photos before the evidence is "fixed" is vital.

step.name: Step 14
step.text: Who was working the neighboring station? Who was the first person to reach you after the accident? Collect their names and phone numbers. The insurance company will often try to blame "user error," and having coworkers who can testify that the machine had been acting strangely for weeks can save your case.

step.name: Step 15
step.text: ### 5. Obtain the Machine's Make and Model

step.name: Step 16
step.text: This is a step many workers miss. If the machine was defective, you may have a claim against the manufacturer in addition to your workers' comp claim. To pursue this, your lawyer will need the exact model number, manufacturer name, and maintenance records.

step.name: Step 18
step.text: Machine accidents often lead to permanent disabilities and high-value claims. Insurance companies are more likely to fight these cases to minimize their payout. An experienced attorney at Just Work Comp Law can help you navigate the complex appeals process and ensure your impairment rating is calculated fairly.

step.name: Step 19
step.text: By following these steps, you build a foundation of evidence that is difficult for insurance companies to ignore. Remember, the system is designed to provide you with medical care and wage replacement—don't let a procedural mistake cost you your recovery.
